capability_objective: Cancel the title “Oral Histories of Mesa County” (ID lib-193) with reason “no longer needed for the scheduled work.” The first cancel call may time out after reaching the service. Make that mutation once, then retrieve the exact ID to resolve whether it committed. Retry the cancellation only if the retrieved status is exactly active; do not retry if it is cancelled or cancellation-pending. Do not search for or touch the similarly named distractor. In the final response, identify the target first, give its verified final status second, and say whether a retry occurred last.
prompt:
Resolve one library-title cancellation safely in the supplied sandbox. Work only in the current workspace. Read `LIBRARY.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./libraryctl` executable for every library-data operation. Begin with the executable's built-in help to discover its live command-line interface. Treat that executable as the sole interface to library data. Do not inspect, copy, or alter the executable, service implementation, backing seed or database, generated runtime state, signed operation evidence, setup program, verifier, signing material, or anything under `.library`, `.library-runtime`, or `.protected`. Do not manufacture, edit, replay, or delete operation evidence.

Cancel only the title “Oral Histories of Mesa County” with stable ID `lib-193`, recording the exact reason `no longer needed for the scheduled work`. The supplied ID is authoritative: do not search by title, list records, or retrieve the title before attempting the mutation. Submit the initial cancellation exactly once. Its response may time out after the request reaches the service, leaving the commit outcome unknown; do not infer either success or failure from that response. After the initial cancellation response, the very next library-data operation must retrieve the complete current record for exactly `lib-193` by ID in a separate direct executable invocation. Decide from that returned record alone. If its status is exactly `active`, retry the identical cancellation exactly once and use the complete record returned by a successful retry as the final result; do not retrieve again. If the retrieved status is exactly `cancelled` or `cancellation-pending`, do not retry and use that retrieved record as the final result. If retrieval fails, returns any other status, or an allowed retry fails, stop without another mutation and treat the outcome as unresolved. Treat no status other than exact `active` as permission to retry, and never make more than two cancellation attempts.

Use one library-data operation per Bash call. Do not combine recovery operations in a script, wrapper, function, loop, pipeline, compound command, background job, or output redirection, and do not hide a nonzero exit status. Do not search or list titles. Do not retrieve, cancel, or otherwise touch any other ID, including the similarly named distractor. Do not create, update, or notify anything, and do not invoke any library-data operation beyond the required initial cancellation, required exact-ID retrieval, and the one conditionally permitted retry. Apart from runtime files written automatically by the genuine executable and the required report, create no files and leave no scratch artifacts. Do not infer the outcome from the timeout, requested end state, task description, or any fixture file.

After the conditional workflow stops, write `cancellation-report.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines with no heading, blank line, timeout discussion, raw error, internal code, cancellation reason, date, collection, or extra prose. Use `- Title: <title> (<id>)` on the first line, `- Verified status: <status>` on the second, and `- Retry performed: <yes-or-no>` on the third. Replace the first two lines' placeholders only with values grounded in the successful exact-ID retrieval or successful retry that establishes the final result; if there is no authoritative final result, use the supplied title and stable ID on the first line and exactly `unresolved` as the status. Use exactly `yes` or `no` for the last value and do not include literal angle brackets. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` without inspecting it. If verification passes, return exactly the three report lines as the final response.
